name: Tiny Tim
creator: Charles Dickens
practice: Being the object of pity that motivates a rich man's reform
approach: The disabled child as moral instrument — his suffering exists to teach Scrooge a lesson. His disability is never described medically. His cure is never explained. He exists to make a non-disabled audience feel something.
body_of_work: Tiny Tim appears in Stave 3 of A Christmas Carol. His crutch, his fragility, his 'God bless us, everyone' — all designed to soften Scrooge's heart. Without Tim's disability, Scrooge has no motivation to change.
why_instructive: He's the anti-pattern — 'inspiration porn.' Using disabled people as motivation for non-disabled people. The dome must never instrumentalize disability. A person's health layer serves THEM, not the system's narrative about redemption.

dome_relevance.4: Health — disability as narrative device rather than lived experience
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy — zero autonomy, exists entirely for others' moral education
